front: Short Story: the narrator fails to buy a gift for Mangan's sister at the title bazaar and sees himself as 'a creature driven and derided by vanity'
Short Story: Bartell D'Arcy sings 'The Lass of Aughrim'; Gretta Conroy remembers Michael Furey; ends with snow falling 'upon all the living and the dead'
Short Story: the title character clutches an iron railing and decides against immigrating to Buenos Aires with Frank
Novel: set on June 16, 1904 (Bloomsday); ends with Molly Bloom's 'yes I said yes I will Yes'; opens with 'stately, plump Buck Mulligan'
Novel: 'Oxen of the Sun' recapitulates the history of English prose as Mina Purefoy gives birth; 'Circe' is set in Nighttown's brothel
Novel: begins 'riverrun, past Eve and Adam's'; circular structure; coined the word 'quark'; hundred-letter thunder words; ALP and HCE
Novel: Stephen Dedalus attends Jesuit schools; Father Arnall's hell sermon traumatizes him; he vows to forge 'the uncreated conscience of my race'
back: A Portrait of the Artist as a Young Man (James Joyce)
work: A Portrait of the Artist as a Young Man

front: Author: infamously filthy love letters to Nora Barnacle sold for 240,000 pounds at Sotheby's; their first date (June 16) became Bloomsday
